title: analytics — model relationships
description: Four owned entities with a tight internal graph. External graph is intentionally minimal — analytics is a leaf module in the growth tree. Downstream consumers (marketing, finance, referrals, notifications) subscribe to AnalyticsEvent* events; they do NOT hold foreign keys back to this module. Attribution snapshots are frozen jsonb, not FKs — attribution changes don't ripple into historical events.

internal.description: Restrict on delete — events must not vanish while deliveries reference them. PurgeExpiredAnalyticsDataJob purges deliveries FIRST (90-day window), then events (2-year window).
Set-null on delete — historical deliveries survive when a config is deleted. The `provider` denormalised column preserves the identity of the driver used.
Reverse of AnalyticsDelivery.belongsTo. One event has N deliveries (one per provider × attempt).
Reverse of AnalyticsDelivery.belongsTo. One config has N deliveries.

outbound.description: Every analytics event is tenant-scoped. On tenant erasure the cascade runs PurgeAnalyticsDataForErasedTenant which writes a summary row into audit + hard-deletes every event + delivery + identity.

Polymorphic subject. Identified events point at User; anonymous events point at AnalyticsIdentity. When identify() merges anonymous → user, MergeAliasedEventsJob rewrites historical subject_id/subject_type from AnalyticsIdentity to User.
Every provider config is tenant-scoped. Cross-tenant configs (rare, super_admin) refused by AnalyticsProviderConfigObserver.
Set-null preserves the identity trail after user erasure. The row transitions to `deidentified=true` via observer AND the identified_user_id column nulls.

inbound.description: Analytics is a leaf in the growth graph. Downstream modules (marketing, finance, referrals, notifications, planned Wave 5+) subscribe to AnalyticsEvent* published events; they hold NO foreign keys back to this module. AnalyticsEventCapturer classes register as listeners via #[AsAnalyticsEventCapturer] but the capturer implementations LIVE in the consumer module — they read domain-event context and produce analytics-event payloads.
consumer_patterns: any module with a listener class carrying #[AsAnalyticsEventCapturer(domainEvent: X::class, analyticsEventName: 'y_z')] — the listener becomes a registered capturer in AnalyticsEventCapturerRegistry
any module subscribing to `analytics.event.delivered` webhook events — cross-service consumption of analytics deliveries via the webhook module
consumer_examples_planned: marketing — subscribes to AnalyticsFunnelStepReached to correlate funnel completion against marketing attribution (ROAS query support)
finance — emits domain events (SubscriptionUpgraded, PaymentSucceeded) that a finance-owned AnalyticsEventCapturer maps to 'subscription_upgraded' + 'payment_succeeded' analytics events
referrals — emits ReferralConversionRecorded which a referrals-owned capturer maps to 'referral_converted' with the referrer + referee properties

notifications — emits NotificationOpened / Clicked which a notifications-owned capturer maps to 'notification_opened' + 'notification_clicked' analytics events with { channel, template_key, provider } properties

consumed.usage: Every entity is tenant-scoped via BelongsToTenant. No exceptions — there are no platform-level analytics rows.
Polymorphic subject on AnalyticsEvent (post-identify) + belongsTo target of AnalyticsIdentity.identified_user_id.
Application ID cascades through Tenant — no direct FK. Every analytics event participates in an application via tenants.application_id join.
AnalyticsEventObserver.creating snapshots the current AttributionContext into AnalyticsEvent.attribution jsonb. Immutable after CREATE.

AnalyticsEventObserver.creating calls ConsentGate::has(subject, 'analytics') to determine eligible_provider_count. AnalyticsProviderManager re-checks at dispatch time via consent_snapshot on the event row (a snapshot NOT a fresh call — that lets us honor the consent state that was in effect when the event was captured, even if the user's consent has since changed).

analytics_capture (module master gate). analytics_provider_slot (config-create quota). analytics_event_slot_per_month (dispatch pool). analytics_advanced_providers (Mixpanel/June/Segment). analytics_batch_configuration (custom batch_config). analytics_identity_merge (identity merge routes). analytics_sampling_config (custom sampling_rate). analytics_extended_retention (2y → 5y events). analytics_session_recording (PostHog session recording opt-in).
Four notification categories seeded under the `analytics` namespace for circuit-breaker + backpressure + drop-rate + identity-merge notifications.
